what_it_does: A zero-dependency Python static-file and JSON API server for a design-DNA gallery. It serves files from the project root, routes GET/POST endpoints for feedback, tokens, grammar, layouts, sequence, grown artifacts, content stories, and any dna/*.json face, and answers CORS preflight requests.
observations: Uses only the Python standard library (http.server, json, os, tempfile).
Serves static files from ROOT, the directory two levels above the script.
Implements GET and POST for /api/feedback, /api/tokens, /api/grammar, /api/layouts, /api/sequence, /api/grown, /api/content, /api/content/<sid>, and /api/dna/<name>.
POST endpoints accept the full JSON body and write it atomically via tempfile + os.replace.
DNA_FACES is built once at import time from files in dna/*.json.
The /api/grown POST endpoint upserts grown specs and also derives a story tree under content/stories and registers it in content/index.json.
CORS headers are emitted on JSON responses and OPTIONS returns 204.
Runs a ThreadingHTTPServer on port 8090 with SO_REUSEADDR and daemon threads.
standouts: The grown endpoint auto-authors a story tree and content-index entry from a Bench spec.
DNA faces are discovered from disk rather than hard-coded.
conventions: REST-ish JSON endpoints layered over SimpleHTTPRequestHandler.
Atomic file writes with tempfile.mkstemp in the target directory.
concerns: DNA_FACES is computed once at import time, so new dna/*.json files are not visible until the server restarts.
No schema validation beyond requiring a JSON object.
No authentication or authorization on POST endpoints.
Static serving exposes the entire project root.
POST /api/grown writes grown.json with indent=1 while other JSON files use indent=2.
Some json.load(open(...)) calls leave file objects to be garbage-collected rather than explicitly closed.
notes: A small stdlib-only dev server that treats JSON files as the design system's source of truth and promotes bench-grown specs into authored content trees.
questions: What is the exact structure expected in a grown spec's 'spec' field?
How are tokens, grammar, layouts, and sequence intended to relate to one another semantically?
Are clients expected to handle the case where content/index.json is missing or malformed?
Is there any intended concurrency control beyond atomic writes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the core behavior, but the file also adds endpoints (layouts, sequence, grown, content, dna faces) and the grown-to-content bridge that are not mentioned in the opening docstring.
apparent_themes: Derive endpoints from disk, never hard-code lists
Promote generated artifacts to authored content
intention_signals: It is trying to be a lightweight, restartable HTTP backplane for a design system where JSON files are the canonical registry, and where bench-generated plans can be kept and immediately surfaced as browsable stories.
novelty: The /api/grown endpoint is unusual: it not only stores a bench-grown spec but also synthesizes an authored story tree and registers it in the content index, blurring the line between generated plans and authored content.
contribution: Provides a zero-dependency HTTP surface for reading and writing JSON design substrates and static assets, with atomic writes, disk-derived endpoint discovery, and a bridge from bench-grown specs to authored content trees.
summary_for_embedding: A Python stdlib static-file and JSON API server for a design-DNA project, exposing GET/POST endpoints for feedback, tokens, grammar, layouts, sequence, grown artifacts, content stories, and dna faces with CORS and atomic file writes.
intention_for_embedding: To give the design system a local, restartable HTTP backplane that treats JSON files as the source of truth and lets generated bench artifacts graduate into authored content.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler class that adds JSON API routes and CORS to SimpleHTTPRequestHandler.
symbols.description: Subclass of SimpleHTTPRequestHandler pinned to serve from ROOT; routes OPTIONS, GET, and POST to API endpoints and falls back to static file serving for everything else.

symbols.does: Initializes the handler with directory fixed to ROOT.
symbols.description: Calls the superclass constructor with directory=ROOT so the served directory is always the project root regardless of the process working directory.

symbols.does: Emits common CORS headers on the response.
symbols.description: Sends Access-Control-Allow-Origin, Access-Control-Allow-Methods, and Access-Control-Allow-Headers headers.

symbols.does: Serializes an object to JSON and sends it as the response body.
symbols.description: Encodes the object as UTF-8 JSON, sends the given HTTP status, sets Content-Type and CORS headers, and writes the body.

symbols.does: Handles CORS preflight by returning HTTP 204.
symbols.description: Responds to any OPTIONS request with a 204 status and CORS headers, no body.

symbols.does: Dispatches GET requests across API endpoints and static files.
symbols.description: Routes root to the gallery HTML, reads JSON endpoints, serves content stories and dna faces, and delegates all other paths to the base static file handler.

symbols.does: Dispatches POST requests to the appropriate JSON write endpoint.
symbols.description: Routes POSTs to feedback, tokens, grammar, layouts, sequence, grown, and dna face endpoints; returns 404 for unrecognized paths.

symbols.does: Returns the filesystem path for the grown artifacts JSON file.
symbols.description: Computes pieces/grown.json relative to ROOT each time it is called.

symbols.does: Returns the current list of grown artifacts.
symbols.description: Reads pieces/grown.json and returns its contents, defaulting to {"grown": []} if the file is missing or malformed.

symbols.does: Derives and writes a story tree from a Bench spec into content/.
symbols.description: Builds a story tree whose root line comes from the spec root and whose children come from the plan pages, writes it to content/stories/<gid>.json, and registers the story in content/index.json.

symbols.does: Upserts a grown spec and triggers the story-tree derivation.
symbols.description: Validates that the body contains id and spec, timestamps it, updates pieces/grown.json, and best-effort writes the corresponding authored story tree and content index entry.

symbols.does: Generic GET handler that returns the parsed contents of a JSON file.
symbols.description: Opens the given path, parses JSON, and sends it; on error returns a 500 JSON error response.

symbols.does: Generic POST handler that atomically overwrites a JSON file.
symbols.description: Parses the request body as JSON, validates it is an object, writes it to a tempfile in the target directory, and replaces the target file atomically.

symbols.does: Returns the current feedback.json state.
symbols.description: Reads state/feedback.json if it exists, otherwise returns {"mockups": {}}.

symbols.does: Atomically overwrites feedback.json with the full request body.
symbols.description: Parses the POST body as a JSON object and writes it atomically to state/feedback.json via tempfile and os.replace.

symbols.does: Returns the current dna/tokens.json.
symbols.description: Reads and returns the canonical stable token set from dna/tokens.json, returning 500 on read or parse errors.

symbols.does: Atomically overwrites dna/tokens.json with the full request body.
symbols.description: Parses the POST body as a JSON object and writes it atomically to dna/tokens.json with two-space indentation.

symbols.does: Returns the current dna/grammar.json.
symbols.description: Reads and returns the generative rule layer from dna/grammar.json, returning 500 on read or parse errors.

symbols.does: Atomically overwrites dna/grammar.json with the full request body.
symbols.description: Parses the POST body as a JSON object and writes it atomically to dna/grammar.json with two-space indentation.

symbols.does: Logs requests to stderr with a minimal format.
symbols.description: Overrides SimpleHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to write a single line containing the client address and request summary to standard error.

symbols.does: ThreadingHTTPServer subclass configured for reuse and daemon threads.
symbols.description: Sets allow_reuse_address and daemon_threads so the server can restart quickly and worker threads do not block shutdown.

symbols.does: Entry point that creates and runs the server on port 8090.
symbols.description: Instantiates Server bound to 0.0.0.0:8090 with Handler, prints a startup message, and serves until interrupted, then closes cleanly.
